title: Your First Quidditch Match
prose: The whole school turns out for it, scarves and rosettes in house colours, a magically amplified commentary voice rattling off names faster than you can match them to the tiny, distant figures wheeling over the pitch.

It's cold enough that your hands go numb around your cup of hot chocolate, and none of it matters the second the Snitch is spotted — the whole stand lurches forward as one, shouting, as two Seekers dive nearly vertically with the crowd's breath held in a single collective knot.

Whichever way the final whistle goes, the walk back up to the castle afterward is loud with replays and arguments about a call the referee definitely got wrong, and for a little while the whole school agrees on nothing quite so completely as Quidditch.

choices.label: Your house takes it, in the end.
A close loss — there's always the next match.
